question: What is the chemical and volume control system (CVCS) role in the NuScale RCS?

nuscale_ch05_0012.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Leakage Detection
nuscale_ch05_0012.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) does not use traditional components with designed leakage rates, making it impractical to distinguish between identified and unidentified leakage inside containment using installed instrumentation. All leakage into containment is treated as unidentified until the source is determined, with leakage rates monitored through RCS inventory balance and containment evacuation system (CES) flow rates, and operational limits set in plant technical specifications. The leakage detection systems are designed to be reliable, redundant, and sensitive enough to support leak-before-break analyses.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain fuel integrity, minimize solid deposits, and ensure uniform boron concentration under all operating conditions, supporting both performance and safety requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located inside the containment vessel and includes components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. The RCS removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and features systems for removing noncondensible gases and maintaining pressure boundary integrity to prevent radionuclide release. It interfaces with several other systems, including emergency core cooling and chemical control, to ensure safe and efficient operation.

nuscale_ch05_0007.title: Integrity of Reactor Coolant Boundary
nuscale_ch05_0007.summary: This section describes the design features of each NuScale Power Module (NPM) that ensure the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) throughout the plant’s design life. It details the components and systems included in the RCPB, such as the reactor pressure vessel, containment isolation valves, safety valves, and associated piping, all designed in accordance with regulatory definitions and requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0002.title: Summary Description
nuscale_ch05_0002.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping to transfer heat from the reactor core to the steam generator, ensuring safe and efficient operation. The RCS design supports up to twelve modules, maintains proper coolant chemistry and boron concentration, and is engineered to meet performance and safety requirements during normal and off-normal conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0010.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Materials
nuscale_ch05_0010.summary: The Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary (RCPB) materials, including welds, are selected and fabricated in accordance with ASME BPVC standards and meet all relevant regulatory requirements, including those in 10 CFR 50. These materials are designed, constructed, and tested to ensure high quality, environmental compatibility, and fracture resistance, with specific material specifications detailed in Table 5.2-4. Quality assurance measures are also established for on-site handling during construction.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer in the NuScale reactor is integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and functions to regulate reactor coolant system (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering unnecessary reactor trips or safety system actuations. Instrumentation provides continuous monitoring of pressure and water level to support safe operation.

nuscale_ch05_0009.title: Overpressure Protection
nuscale_ch05_0009.summary: This section outlines NuScale's compliance with NRC regulations and ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) requirements for overpressure protection of the reactor coolant boundary. It details the use of specific ASME code cases for design, fabrication, construction, preservice, and inservice inspections and testing, ensuring all selected code cases meet NRC-endorsed conditions as specified in relevant regulatory guides and federal regulations. Table 5.2-1 consolidates the specific code cases applied.

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) comply with relevant regulatory requirements, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria, by designing, fabricating, and testing reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) Class 1 standards. It also clarifies the classification of connected piping and states that the 2013 Edition of the ASME BPVC is the code of record for the NuScale design certification.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the NuScale reactor pressurizer, specifying the alloys and standards for each component, such as the baffle plate, heater bundles, cover plate, and bolting. It emphasizes the use of corrosion-resistant materials compliant with ASME BPVC Section II specifications to ensure compatibility and durability in the reactor coolant system.

edges.description: Section A discusses the inability to distinguish certain types of RCS leakage due to the absence of traditional components, which is a direct consequence of the RCS design described in Section B that omits pumps and external piping.

Section A discusses the challenges in detecting RCS leakage due to the absence of traditional leakage paths, while Section B describes RCS features (like degasification and pressure-retaining components) that influence how leakage and system integrity are managed, connecting both sections through their focus on RCS boundary integrity and leakage management.

Section A discusses the detection of leakage from the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB), which is a subset of the broader integrity requirements for the RCPB described in Section B, connecting them through the shared focus on maintaining and monitoring RCPB integrity in the NuScale RCS.

Section A provides a general overview of the RCS design and its reliance on natural circulation, while Section B specifies particular RCS features (degasification line, reactor vent valves) that support the system's operational functions described in Section A.

Section A describes specific RCS features (such as degasification and pressure-retaining components) that contribute to maintaining the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, which is the focus of Section B's regulatory requirement.

Section A provides a general overview of the RCS design and its reliance on natural circulation, while Section B discusses the implications of this design—specifically, the absence of traditional leakage paths and the challenges in detecting RCS boundary leakage, thus Section B elaborates on operational consequences of the design described in Section A.

Section A describes the pressurizer's design and function as part of the RCS, while Section B discusses leakage detection in the RCS, with both sections connected by their focus on RCS integrity and pressure management.

Section A specifies the regulatory and code compliance requirements for the reactor coolant pressure boundary, which includes components described in Section B's design basis summary of the RCS.

Section A establishes that the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components, including those relevant to the CVCS, comply with regulatory codes, while Section B describes the RCS features (such as degasification and pressure retention) that the CVCS supports to maintain RCPB integrity.
